title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on improving platform reliability and data quality for RevOps teams. OpsPal now automatically maintains cleaner data by removing outdated configurations and preventing common Salesforce setup errors. The platform also runs more smoothly with better startup performance and automatic dependency management.

entries.headline: Enhanced data validation and quality checks
details: Stricter validation ensures data transformations produce consistent results
Advisory-only recommendations no longer block your workflows
Faster processing when running unrelated commands

entries.headline: Automatic platform maintenance during updates
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned sessions after 5 minutes
Warns when memory usage approaches limits

entries.headline: Better cleanup of outdated platform components
details: Works across all operating systems including Windows
Cleans up components from all installed plugins

entries.headline: Automatic removal of outdated plugins at startup
Better Salesforce startup and quality monitoring
details: Improved workflow processing for Salesforce connections
Enhanced quality and architecture monitoring for Salesforce orgs

entries.headline: Resolved critical Salesforce configuration issues
details: Prevents field addition errors when Dynamic Forms are active
Automatically assigns record types to avoid permission errors
Reduces workflow blocking from routing decisions

entries.headline: Automatic dependency management at startup
details: Installs missing dependencies automatically when safe
Shows clear error messages when manual fixes are needed
Provides specific commands to resolve dependency issues

entries.headline: Resolved false missing package errors at startup
